original_hypothesis.title: Misconfiguration lock: incorrect initialization of configStorage address bricks LandManager via _reconfigure external calls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: initialize(address _configStorage) is the sole entry to set configStorage (inherited) and then calls _reconfigure(), which immediately dereferences configStorage to pull multiple addresses/uints/arrays. If _configStorage is set incorrectly (EOA, wrong contract, wrong chain instance), LandManager can be bricked (reverts on config reads) and any already-staked NFTs become stuck because all key user functions are gated by notPaused and depend on configured dependencies.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this is a deployment/config risk (not a proxy frontrun), it is a realistic operational failure mode with severe custody impact if initialization is performed with an incorrect address or if configStorage is later upgraded to an incompatible implementation.
deployment_assumptions: Initializer is called with an incorrect _configStorage address, or configStorage is later replaced/upgraded to an incompatible contract that breaks getAddress/getUint/getSmall*Array semantics
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script for LandManager initialization was provided; only generic proxy upgrade scripts exist and do not demonstrate initialize() argument correctness.

proof_of_signal.pattern: initialize calls _reconfigure which performs multiple external reads on configStorage and assigns critical dependency addresses/rates

preconditions: LandManager is deployed behind a proxy and initialize() is called with wrong _configStorage, or configStorage changes break expected interface
Users have already staked NFTs (custody in LandManager) or will attempt to stake/farm/unstake after misconfiguration
No separate recovery/admin withdrawal exists in LandManager

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: hard dependency wiring without sanity checks
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: miswired managers cause systemic revert/DoS and custody lock-in
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no validation that resolved addresses are contracts/nonzero; no PRICE_PER_PLOT > 0 check; no rescue

analysis_questions: Does BaseBlastManagerUpgradeable validate configStorage is a contract and supports required selectors?
Are there deployment scripts that atomically deploy and initialize with verified addresses?
Is there an admin remediation path if dependencies are miswired (pause + rescue)?
validation_requirements: Review deployment pipeline for LandManagerProxy + initialize calldata
Chaos-test configUpdated with bad config values and observe user exit feasibility

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Operational mistake sets _configStorage to a wrong address; _reconfigure reverts or sets nonsense dependency addresses; thereafter, users cannot successfully farm/unstake and NFTs can become stuck.
prerequisites: Privileged operational error or compromised configuration process

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Protocol-wide outage; potential permanent lock of custodied NFTs until upgrade/fix
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Medium (requires correct reconfiguration or upgrade; may still be blocked if calls revert)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing deployment-simulation tests validating config wiring and recovery from bad configs.

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor configUpdated/initialize tx args and validate resolved dependency codehashes

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
